citations.description: search_files for 'merge --ff-only origin/main' in AGENTS.md returned no matches
search_files for 'shared main checkout' in AGENTS.md returned no matches
search_files for 'fast-forward' in AGENTS.md returned no matches

findings.criterion: AC1: AGENTS.md carries the post-merge fast-forward-your-worktree guidance AND the 'never touch the shared main checkout' warning.
findings.detail: Neither 'merge --ff-only origin/main' nor 'shared main checkout' (case-insensitive) appear anywhere in AGENTS.md. The ticket comment claims edits were pushed to Gerrit change 827 but the change has not been merged into the repository's main branch.

citations.description: search_files for 'merge --ff-only origin/main' in CONTRIBUTING.md returned no matches
search_files for 'shared main checkout' in CONTRIBUTING.md returned no matches
search_files for '2f.' in CONTRIBUTING.md returned no matches

findings.criterion: AC2: CONTRIBUTING.md's landing recipe carries the same two-part guidance in a new §2f.
findings.detail: None of the three required strings appear in CONTRIBUTING.md: 'merge --ff-only origin/main' is absent, 'shared main checkout' is absent, and '2f.' is absent. The §2f subsection does not exist in the file.

citations.description: AC1 and AC2 searches confirm the changes are not in the current repo HEAD, so the merge has not landed

findings.criterion: AC3: ZERO code changes — the diff touches only Markdown. Verify: git diff --name-only origin/main HEAD lists only *.md paths.
findings.detail: Since the documented changes (AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md) are not present in the repository, the Gerrit change 827 has not been merged. AC3 cannot be verified as passed; the diff cannot be confirmed to list only markdown paths from the current HEAD.

citations.description: Ticket comment states 'AC4 make lint exits 0' but this is asserted against the unmerged Gerrit branch, not the repo HEAD

findings.criterion: AC4: The pre-commit gate passes on the change. Verify: make lint exits 0.
findings.detail: This criterion requires the actual change to be present and lint to pass against it. Since the changes are not merged into the repository, this cannot be verified as complete. The ticket comment claims make lint exits 0 against the Gerrit branch, but the code is not in the repo.

citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart: 'Pushed to Gerrit as change 827; polling LLM-Review+1 AND Verified+1 before Submit' — describes pending state, not MERGED
Gerrit change 827 referenced in ticket comment — MERGED status not confirmed

findings.criterion: AC5 [operator-attested]: the change lands via Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1, then Submit/MERGED) and the ticket is closed from the FAST-FORWARDED worktree, without the shared main checkout being touched.
findings.detail: The ticket comment references Gerrit change 827 at https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/827 and says it is 'polling LLM-Review+1 AND Verified+1 before Submit'. This is not an attestation of MERGED status — it describes an in-progress state. No comment records the MERGED status, no confirmation of worktree-only close is present. The required specifics (MERGED status, worktree-only close confirmation) are absent.
findings.remediation: Record a new ticket comment confirming: (1) the Gerrit change number (827) reached MERGED status with LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1 — include the timestamp and final status from Gerrit; (2) confirmation that the ticket was closed from the fast-forwarded worktree only, with no touch to the shared main checkout.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
